project.name: NextRole
project.description: NextRole is a GenAI career assistant that turns a CV and a target job description into a tailored resume PDF, a researched interview-prep doc, and a day-of battlecard, produced by a multi-agent system with long-term memory.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes backend.agents an importable Python package.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker that makes backend.agents.career_agent an importable package.

nodes.name: agents.py
nodes.summary: Wires and instantiates the career deep-agent: applies prompt overrides, builds the CompositeBackend (virtual-path shell + KV StoreBackend routes + object-store artifact routes), constructs the tool set, and assembles the main agent plus declarative subagents.
nodes.languageNotes: Monkeypatches deepagents/langchain prompt constants (module attrs plus frozen __kwdefaults__) before create_deep_agent().

nodes.name: middleware.py
nodes.summary: Custom LangChain agent middleware for the career agent: injects the current UTC date, seeds the always-loaded preferences file, and overrides the model (and optionally disables subagent streaming) per run.

nodes.name: object_backend.py
nodes.summary: Implements the deepagents BackendProtocol over S3-compatible object storage for a single artifact area, mapping virtual paths to scoped object keys and returning errors in-band.
nodes.languageNotes: Storage-only backend (not a SandboxBackendProtocol) so execute() keeps routing to the shell backend; binary reads return base64 FileData blocks.

nodes.name: object_storage.py
nodes.summary: Object-store settings, virtual-path to object-key mapping, and byte helpers for binary artifacts, shared by the agent's ObjectStoreBackend and the files HTTP API so the path-to-key mapping exists exactly once.
nodes.languageNotes: Object keys are a pure function of virtual path plus caller scope (users/<scope>/career_agent/...) with no DB registry; obstore S3Store speaks S3/GCS/Azure.

nodes.name: scope.py
nodes.summary: Per-user scoping of the agent's persisted artifacts: resolves the caller's identity from the LangGraph run config and derives KV-store namespaces and object-key scope prefixes, falling back to single-user layout when unauthenticated.
nodes.languageNotes: Dual-mode invariant: with no custom auth, identity is None and both storage tiers collapse to their byte-for-byte single-user form.

nodes.name: shell_backend.py
nodes.summary: Virtual-path-aware shell backend: subclasses deepagents LocalShellBackend to rewrite /virtual/path tokens in commands to real on-disk paths, and builds a minimal PATH-only env that keeps secrets out of subprocesses.

nodes.name: tools.py
nodes.summary: Defines the career agent's tools: Tavily web search/extract, backend-bound file list/overwrite, LlamaParse document parsing, JD extraction, and the resume (rendercv) and battlecard (weasyprint) PDF render pipelines.
nodes.languageNotes: Tool factories close over the shared CompositeBackend; heavy native deps (weasyprint, llama_cloud) are imported lazily to keep module import safe.

nodes.name: utils.py
nodes.summary: Loads declarative subagent definitions from YAML and wires each one's tools by name against a caller-owned pool, threading in default tools and middleware so subagents never silently inherit the main agent's full tool set.

nodes.name: files_api.py
nodes.summary: Starlette artifact-files HTTP API (list/read/upload/write/delete) mounted into the agent server via LANGGRAPH_HTTP; speaks virtual paths, scopes keys to the request user, and runs sync obstore calls in worker threads.
nodes.languageNotes: Response shapes preserved from the former Next.js /api/files/* routes; belt-and-braces 401 guard for multi-user mode.

nodes.name: _apply_prompt_overrides
nodes.summary: Replaces the prompt constants that deepagents/langchain inject into the system message, patching both module attributes and the frozen __init__.__kwdefaults__ for middlewares that capture their prompt as a default arg.

nodes.name: UtcDatetimeMiddleware
nodes.summary: AgentMiddleware that appends a 'Current UTC date' line to the system message per model call so the agent can interpret file modification timestamps without invalidating prompt caches every turn.

nodes.name: EnsurePreferencesFileMiddleware
nodes.summary: AgentMiddleware that seeds the always-loaded /memory/preferences.md scaffold in before_agent (idempotent, no model round-trip) because the model reliably appends but not creates that file.

nodes.name: ModelOverrideMiddleware
nodes.summary: AgentMiddleware that overrides the request model from configurable keys (main_agent_model / subagent_model) and, as a rollback lever, disables streaming for subagent calls; distinguishes subagent vs main via metadata.lc_agent_name.

nodes.name: _resolve_model
nodes.summary: Builds and memoizes a chat model from a 'provider:model' string, returning None on any failure so callers fall back to the bake-time default instead of crashing the run.

nodes.name: _without_streaming
nodes.summary: Returns a copy of a chat model with token streaming disabled (via model_copy so the shared cached instance is untouched), used as the subagent-streaming rollback path.

nodes.name: ObjectStoreBackend
nodes.summary: Storage-only deepagents backend for one object-store artifact area, implementing ls/read/write/edit/glob/grep plus upload/download over S3-compatible storage with scoped keys and in-band error results.

nodes.name: ObjectStoreSettings
nodes.summary: Pydantic settings model for the artifact object store, read from OBJECT_STORE_* env vars with all fields defaulted so importing modules never require storage env.

nodes.name: build_store_from_settings
nodes.summary: Constructs an obstore S3Store from ObjectStoreSettings, raising when required connection fields are missing.

nodes.name: get_store
nodes.summary: LRU-cached factory returning the env-configured object store, validating configuration on first use.

nodes.name: _safe_relative
nodes.summary: Normalizes and validates a user-supplied path into a safe relative POSIX key segment, rejecting absolute paths and '..' traversal.

nodes.name: area_key_prefix
nodes.summary: Returns the scoped object-key prefix for an artifact area (users/<scope>/career_agent/<area>/).

nodes.name: key_for_area
nodes.summary: Maps an area name and area-relative path to a full scoped object key, returning None when the path is unsafe.

nodes.name: key_for_virtual_path
nodes.summary: Maps a virtual path (e.g. /upload/cv.pdf) to a scoped object key, allow-listing the leading area segment and rejecting traversal.

nodes.name: virtual_path_for_key
nodes.summary: Inverse mapping from a scoped object key back to its virtual path, stripping the scope prefix.

nodes.name: get_bytes
nodes.summary: Fetches an object's bytes by key, returning None when the key is missing rather than raising.

nodes.name: delete_key
nodes.summary: Deletes an object by key and reports whether it existed beforehand.

nodes.name: current_identity
nodes.summary: Resolves the authenticated caller's identity from the LangGraph run config, returning None in single-user mode or outside a runnable context.

nodes.name: kv_namespace
nodes.summary: Builds the KV-store namespace tuple for an area, prepending the caller identity only when present so single-user namespaces keep their original 2-tuple form.

nodes.name: object_scope
nodes.summary: Returns the object-key scope prefix (users/<identity>/career_agent), mapping absent identity to 'default' to preserve the single-user layout.

nodes.name: default_shell_env
nodes.summary: Builds a minimal PATH-only environment (interpreter bin dir first) for agent shell commands so console scripts like rendercv resolve while keeping provider API keys out of subprocesses.

nodes.name: VirtualPathShellBackend
nodes.summary: LocalShellBackend subclass that rewrites /virtual/path tokens in a command to their real on-disk paths under root_dir, leaving genuine absolute paths and '..' escapes untouched.

nodes.name: web_search
nodes.summary: Agent tool that searches the web for current information via the Tavily client.

nodes.name: web_extract
nodes.summary: Agent tool that extracts full page content from URLs via the Tavily client.

nodes.name: make_list_files
nodes.summary: Factory building the list_files tool (closed over the backend) that lists a directory with size and modification time newest-first, normalizing a missing directory to an empty list.

nodes.name: _upsert
nodes.summary: Writes content to a path with overwrite semantics by trying write() and falling back to edit() on the already-exists error, since the BackendProtocol refuses overwrite and has no delete.

nodes.name: make_overwrite_file
nodes.summary: Factory building the overwrite_file tool (closed over the backend) that upserts file content at a path.

nodes.name: _materialize_source
nodes.summary: Materializes a backend source path (object-store or virtual) to a real temp file on disk so external parsers can read it, returning an error string on failure.

nodes.name: make_parse_document
nodes.summary: Factory building the parse_document tool that parses a document via LlamaParse (agentic tier) and persists the resulting markdown to an output path, stripping broken inline image references.

nodes.name: _tavily_extract_one
nodes.summary: Extracts a single URL via Tavily and returns (title, raw_markdown), raising on transport errors or empty results.

nodes.name: _coerce_string_entry
nodes.summary: Coerces a resume-YAML list entry that should be a string (but arrived as a number or a single-key mapping from an unquoted colon) back into a clean string.

nodes.name: _repair_string_entries
nodes.summary: Repairs a list of resume-YAML string entries in place, counting how many were fixed.

nodes.name: _normalize_resume_yaml
nodes.summary: Parses and normalizes rendercv resume YAML, auto-repairing invalid string entries (unquoted colons, bare numbers) and re-dumping, passing unparseable YAML through untouched.

nodes.name: _read_resume_yaml_body
nodes.summary: Reads the stored resume YAML from the backend and strips any trailing rendercv settings block so the durable artifact stays free of machine-local paths.

nodes.name: _write_render_copy
nodes.summary: Writes a scratch render copy of the resume YAML into a temp directory with the canonical rendercv settings block appended, returning the file path and the count of auto-repaired entries.

nodes.name: _run_rendercv
nodes.summary: Runs the rendercv CLI on the render-copy YAML via the shell backend, raising a stage error with the CLI output on failure.

nodes.name: _collect_render_outputs
nodes.summary: Collects the .pdf (and optional .typ) bytes produced by rendercv from the temp render directory, raising a verify error if the expected PDF is missing.

nodes.name: _publish_render_outputs
nodes.summary: Publishes the rendered PDF/typ outputs back to the artifact store next to the source YAML via the backend, naming the stage on failure.

nodes.name: make_render_resume_pdf
nodes.summary: Factory building the render_resume_pdf tool that runs the whole resume pipeline: read YAML, write a settings-bearing render copy in a temp dir, run rendercv, and publish outputs next to the YAML.

nodes.name: _weasyprint_pdf_bytes
nodes.summary: Renders an HTML string to PDF bytes via weasyprint (imported lazily), raising on empty output.

nodes.name: make_render_battlecard_pdf
nodes.summary: Factory building the render_battlecard_pdf tool that reads a battlecard JSON, renders the bundled Jinja2 template to HTML, converts it to a PDF via weasyprint, and publishes it next to the JSON.

nodes.name: make_extract_jd
nodes.summary: Factory building the extract_jd tool that fetches a job-description URL via Tavily, strips broken image refs, and persists the extracted markdown to the backend.

nodes.name: load_subagents
nodes.summary: Loads subagent definitions from YAML and wires each one's tools by name against a caller-owned pool, threading in default tools and middleware and always emitting an explicit tools key.

nodes.name: _authenticated
nodes.summary: Decorator that rejects unauthenticated requests with a 401 when multi-user auth is enabled (belt-and-braces for the misconfigured case).

nodes.name: _scope
nodes.summary: Returns the request user's object-store scope (identity), or None for the single-user default layout.

nodes.name: list_files
nodes.summary: GET /files/list handler that collects object metadata across the requested (allow-listed) area prefixes and returns path/size/isBinary/modifiedAt entries sorted by path.

nodes.name: read_file
nodes.summary: GET /files/read handler that returns a file's content as utf-8 or base64 depending on its extension, 404-ing on a missing key.

nodes.name: upload_files
nodes.summary: POST /files/upload handler that validates multipart uploads (extension allow-list, size cap, target dir) and writes each accepted file to the object store, reporting per-file successes and errors.

nodes.name: write_file
nodes.summary: PUT /files/write handler that writes utf-8 or base64-decoded content to a virtual path in the object store.

nodes.name: delete_file
nodes.summary: DELETE /files/delete handler that deletes an object by virtual path, returning 404 when it was absent.

nodes.name: test_object_storage_integration.py

nodes.name: test_scope.py

nodes.name: test_shell_backend.py

nodes.languageNotes: Falls back to a thread-local AsyncConnectionPool so worker threads without the main-thread pool still obtain connections.

nodes.name: lifespan.py
nodes.summary: ASGI lifespan orchestrator that starts the server's subsystems (Postgres pool, checkpointer, run queue, store TTL sweeper, cron scheduler, gRPC, metrics) in order and tears them down in reverse, with a hard-exit watchdog on shutdown.

nodes.name: long_query_monitor.py
nodes.summary: Background loop that periodically scans pg_stat_activity for long-running queries and logs them, coordinating across workers via a Redis lock and last-scan timestamp.

nodes.name: metrics.py
nodes.summary: Defines the worker-pool metrics data models and a get_metrics helper reporting active/available/max run-queue workers.

nodes.name: migrate.py
nodes.summary: Standalone migration entry point that opens a Postgres pool and applies schema and pgvector-index migrations; used to exercise the Go core server in tests.

nodes.name: queue.py
nodes.summary: The run queue: dispatches queued runs to a pool of background event-loop worker threads, tracks in-flight workers, fires completion webhooks, and coordinates graceful drain on shutdown.

nodes.name: redis.py
nodes.summary: Redis client factory and lifecycle for the runtime, with Redis Cluster pub/sub across slots and a GCP IAM credential provider for authenticated managed Redis.

nodes.name: retry.py
nodes.summary: Provides the retry_db decorator that retries async DB/gRPC operations on transient errors (operational/internal errors, pool timeouts, retryable gRPC status codes, undefined-table races).

nodes.name: routes.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder for the runtime's optional internal HTTP routes hook; currently a no-op stub (get_internal_routes = None).

nodes.name: store.py
nodes.summary: Postgres-backed long-term key/value store (PGSTore) with pgvector index setup and Redis-coordinated TTL sweeping, exposed as a lazily-initialized singleton via Store().

nodes.name: connect
nodes.summary: Async context manager yielding a pooled Postgres connection, transparently opening a thread-local pool for non-main worker threads.

nodes.name: create_pool
nodes.summary: Builds the AsyncConnectionPool from the configured DSN with sizing, timeouts, and per-connection configure/reset hooks.

nodes.name: create_conn
nodes.summary: Opens a single standalone async Postgres connection with JSON serde configured, used outside the pool.

nodes.name: migrate
nodes.summary: Applies pending versioned SQL migrations under a version-tracking table, reading and splitting migration files and recording the highest applied version.

nodes.name: start_pool
nodes.summary: Startup routine that boots Redis, opens the pool, applies store config, and runs migrations under a Redis lock, then launches the pool stats loop.

nodes.name: stop_pool
nodes.summary: Shutdown routine that cancels the stats task, closes the pool (thread-local or global), and stops Redis.

nodes.name: migrate_vector_index
nodes.summary: Sets up the pgvector index by lazily importing the store module and delegating to its vector-index migration.

nodes.name: lifespan
nodes.summary: Async lifespan context manager that brings the full server up (pool, checkpointer, ingestion loop, HTTP/UI, gRPC, metrics, store sweeper, query monitor, run queue, cron) and tears it down in reverse on exit.

nodes.name: queue_with_signal
nodes.summary: Runs the queue worker loop and, on unexpected exit or error, triggers a delayed hard process exit so the container is restarted.

nodes.name: _exit_after_flush
nodes.summary: Schedules a watchdog timer that force-exits the process if graceful shutdown and log flushing do not complete within a timeout.

nodes.name: long_query_monitor_loop
nodes.summary: Periodic loop that, gated by a Redis lock and scan interval, queries active long-running Postgres statements and logs them for observability.

nodes.name: _get_long_running_queries
nodes.summary: Queries pg_stat_activity for statements exceeding a duration threshold and returns normalized, JSON-serializable rows.

nodes.name: get_metrics
nodes.summary: Assembles the worker-pool metrics snapshot (max/active/available workers) from the run queue's worker registry.

nodes.name: migrate_for_tests
nodes.summary: Opens a Postgres pool against the migrations directory and applies schema plus pgvector-index migrations; entry point for exercising the Go core server in tests.

nodes.name: BgLoopRunner
nodes.summary: Runs a dedicated asyncio event loop on its own thread, submitting coroutines to it and draining/closing the pool, store, and gRPC client on context exit.

nodes.name: queue
nodes.summary: Core run-dispatch loop: pulls the next run from Postgres, assigns it a background loop runner, spawns the worker coroutine, tracks in-flight workers and webhooks, and drains everything on shutdown.

nodes.name: shutdown_queue
nodes.summary: Cancels in-flight worker and webhook futures and waits for them to settle within a timeout during queue drain.

nodes.name: stats_loop
nodes.summary: Background loop that periodically reports active/max worker gauges to the metrics reporter.

nodes.name: ClusterPubSub
nodes.summary: Redis Cluster pub/sub connection bound to a single node/slot, managing its connection lifecycle, subscribed channels/patterns, and clean teardown.

nodes.name: _GCPIAMCredentialProvider
nodes.summary: Streaming credential provider that mints and refreshes GCP IAM access tokens (sync and async) for authenticating to managed Redis.

nodes.name: _create_redis_client
nodes.summary: Builds the async Redis (or Cluster) client from the configured URI with retry/backoff and optional IAM auth.

nodes.name: start_redis
nodes.summary: Creates the shared Redis client, verifies connectivity with a ping, and launches the Redis stats loop.

nodes.name: redis_stats
nodes.summary: Collects connection-pool statistics across cluster nodes (or the single client) into a RedisPoolStats snapshot.

nodes.name: get_redis
nodes.summary: Returns the shared async Redis client, lazily creating a per-thread client for non-main worker threads.

nodes.name: get_pubsub
nodes.summary: Async context manager yielding a pub/sub subscribed to the given channels/patterns, routing to the correct cluster node by keyslot when clustered.

nodes.name: retry_db
nodes.summary: Decorator that retries an async DB/gRPC callable up to three times on transient failures (operational/internal errors, retryable gRPC codes, undefined-table races).

nodes.name: PGSTore
nodes.summary: Postgres-backed long-term store subclass that routes cursors through the shared pool, batches grouped operations, and performs Redis-coordinated TTL sweeps.

nodes.name: setup_vector_index
nodes.summary: Runs the pgvector index migrations for the store, applying versioned index-creation SQL and embedding-dimension parameters.

nodes.name: Store
nodes.summary: Factory returning the lazily-initialized store singleton, applying store config and starting the store on first use.

nodes.name: event_streaming.py
nodes.summary: HTTP and WebSocket transport routes for the v2 thread-centric event streaming protocol, exposing an SSE stream endpoint, a JSON command endpoint, and full-duplex WebSocket endpoints scoped to a thread.
nodes.languageNotes: Connections are connection-scoped with no cross-connection server state; SSE delivery dedups via a bounded ring buffer to cover the source/session handoff race.

nodes.name: capabilities.py
nodes.summary: Runtime capability probe that verifies the installed langgraph and langchain-core versions expose the symbols required to serve a Protocol v2 streaming run, failing fast with an actionable error otherwise.

nodes.name: constants.py
nodes.summary: Shared constants for the v2 event streaming layer: the run config key, the replay ring-buffer size, the supported channel set, and the default run stream modes.

nodes.name: event_normalizers.py
nodes.summary: Pure helper functions that normalize raw stream event payloads into protocol shapes, extracting text content, mapping run status to lifecycle status, and coercing updates and interrupt data.

nodes.name: namespace.py
nodes.summary: Namespace helpers for the streaming protocol: build stable lookup keys, strip dynamic segment suffixes, parse event names, and test namespace prefix matches.

nodes.name: service.py
nodes.summary: Defines ThreadRunManager, the thread-scoped orchestrator that owns a bound EventStreamingSession, routes protocol commands, and creates or resumes runs for the v2 SSE and WebSocket transports.

nodes.name: session.py
nodes.summary: Defines EventStreamingSession, the per-run protocol session that normalizes raw upstream stream events into protocol frames while managing a replay buffer, subscriptions, namespace lifecycle, and interrupt handling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ContextVars to derive stable, replay-safe protocol event ids from the upstream Go core's Redis-stream entry ids, so client-side dedup survives reconnects and restarts.

nodes.name: state_normalizers.py
nodes.summary: Normalizes LangGraph thread-state payloads into protocol-shaped events, covering message content blocks, tool calls, audio blocks reconstructed from message kwargs, and full state messages.

nodes.name: types.py
nodes.summary: Type barrel that re-exports the langchain_protocol wire types and defines the local Subscription and NamespaceInfo dataclasses used across the event streaming layer.

nodes.name: metrics_datadog.py
nodes.summary: Datadog metrics layer built on OpenTelemetry: defines tiered metric definitions, a tier-filtering OTLP exporter, and the DatadogMetricsReporter singleton with counter, histogram, latency, and gauge instruments.
nodes.languageNotes: The OpenTelemetry SDK import is guarded by a try/except so the module degrades to a no-op reporter when the OTEL packages are absent.

nodes.name: _thread_events
nodes.summary: SSE endpoint handler that validates the EventStreamRequest filter, installs a connection-scoped subscription on a ThreadRunManager, and streams deduplicated protocol events until the thread stream ends.

nodes.name: _thread_command
nodes.summary: JSON request/response handler that parses a single protocol command, dispatches it through a short-lived ThreadRunManager, and returns the command result.

nodes.name: _thread_websocket
nodes.summary: Full-duplex WebSocket handler that binds a ThreadRunManager to the socket, forwards incoming commands, and runs a watchdog that closes the socket when the thread stream completes.

nodes.name: ProtocolV2Capabilities
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass describing whether the runtime satisfies Protocol v2 requirements, listing any missing symbols and rendering an actionable error message.

nodes.name: probe_event_streaming_v2_capabilities
nodes.summary: Probes for the langgraph and langchain-core symbols required to serve a Protocol v2 run and returns a ProtocolV2Capabilities result, memoized via lru_cache.

nodes.name: ProtocolV2UnsupportedError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when the runtime lacks the symbols needed for Protocol v2 streaming, carrying the capabilities detail so callers can return a clear error envelope.

nodes.name: extract_text_content
nodes.summary: Extracts a plain-text string from a message content value that may be a string, a list of content parts, or structured records.

nodes.name: normalize_input_requested_data
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw interrupt payloads into the protocol's input-requested data array.

nodes.name: strip_interrupts_from_values
nodes.summary: Removes interrupt entries from a values payload so interrupts are surfaced only through dedicated input-requested events.

nodes.name: is_prefix_match
nodes.summary: Checks whether a namespace array starts with a given prefix, comparing segments literally and falling back to a dynamic-suffix-stripped comparison.

nodes.name: ThreadRunManager
nodes.summary: Thread-scoped run manager that owns a bound EventStreamingSession, routes protocol commands such as run.start, input.respond, and subscribe/unsubscribe, and creates or resumes runs for the SSE and WebSocket transports.

nodes.name: _checkpoint_id_from_run_start_config
nodes.summary: Extracts the checkpoint id from a run.start runnable config, raising ValueError when the nested configurable structure is malformed.

nodes.name: validate_checkpoint_id_from_runnable_config
nodes.summary: Validates that a run.start config's checkpoint id is a well-formed UUID and returns it as a normalized string.

nodes.name: _multitask_strategy_from_run_start
nodes.summary: Resolves the multitask strategy for a new run from the run.start params, falling back to the default when unset or invalid.

nodes.name: EventStreamingSession
nodes.summary: Per-run protocol session that consumes raw upstream stream events, normalizes them into protocol frames, maintains a bounded replay buffer, tracks subscriptions and namespace lifecycle, and emits lifecycle, interrupt, and tool events.

nodes.name: ResumeGap
nodes.summary: Value type signalling that a reconnect cursor has fallen behind the replay buffer head, triggering a resume_gap protocol error instead of silent truncation.

nodes.name: _synth_event_id
nodes.summary: Derives a stable, replay-safe protocol event id from the current upstream Redis-stream entry id and a per-source envelope counter.

nodes.name: _is_supported_channel
nodes.summary: Returns whether a value is one of the protocol's supported subscription channels; shared by the transport routes and the run manager.

nodes.name: _custom_event_matches
nodes.summary: Determines whether a custom stream event's data name matches a subscription's custom-channel filter.

nodes.name: _coerce_interrupt_requests
nodes.summary: Coerces raw interrupt values into a normalized list of interrupt request records for input-requested events.

nodes.name: normalize_event_streaming_state_payload
nodes.summary: Top-level normalizer that converts a raw LangGraph thread-state payload into the protocol's state-message shape.

nodes.name: normalize_event_streaming_message_content
nodes.summary: Normalizes a message's content and additional_kwargs into protocol content blocks, including audio reconstructed from the kwargs.

nodes.name: normalize_event_streaming_content_block
nodes.summary: Normalizes a single content value into a protocol content block, mapping recognized types and wrapping unknown ones as non-standard blocks.

nodes.name: _normalize_state_message
nodes.summary: Normalizes a single state message record into the protocol shape, resolving its role and type, content blocks, and tool calls.

nodes.name: _normalize_state_tool_calls
nodes.summary: Normalizes a message's tool-call entries into protocol tool-call blocks, deriving each call's identity and arguments.

nodes.name: _normalize_audio_block_from_additional_kwargs
nodes.summary: Reconstructs a protocol audio content block from a message's additional_kwargs, inferring the MIME type from the audio format.

nodes.name: Subscription
nodes.summary: Dataclass representing an active WebSocket subscription within a session: its id, channel set, optional namespace filters, depth, and active flag.

nodes.name: NamespaceInfo
nodes.summary: Dataclass caching lifecycle metadata for a namespace: its segments, status, and derived graph name.

nodes.name: DatadogMetricsReporter
nodes.summary: Singleton reporter that lazily creates OpenTelemetry instruments and records counters, histograms, latencies, and gauges to Datadog, honoring each metric's emitting tier.

nodes.name: _FilteringExporter
nodes.summary: OTLP metric exporter wrapper that drops metrics above the configured emitting tier before delegating export to the underlying exporter.

nodes.name: MetricDef
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass describing a metric's type, name, and emitting tier; the value type for all module-level metric definitions.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the gRPC bridge subpackage that exposes Python-side services (checkpointing, encryption) to the Go core server.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: Standalone asyncio gRPC server running alongside the Starlette HTTP server; registers the checkpointer, encryption, and health servicers so the Go core server can call into Python.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses grpc.aio for a fully async server with a module-global instance and task for shutdown coordination.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel that re-exports the Python gRPC servicer implementations (CheckpointerServicerImpl, EncryptionServicerImpl) for the server to register.

nodes.name: checkpointer.py
nodes.summary: Implements the Checkpointer gRPC service, converting protos to/from LangGraph checkpoint types and delegating persistence (put, list, get, delete, copy, prune) to the Python checkpointer so the Go server can use Python-based checkpoint storage.

nodes.name: encryption.py
nodes.summary: Implements the Encryption gRPC service, recursively encrypting/decrypting JSON payloads with nested-subfield and skip-field handling so the Go server can delegate to Python custom encryption.

nodes.name: queue_entrypoint.py
nodes.summary: Async worker entrypoint that boots the selected queue runtime, runs a Starlette health/metrics sidecar server, and coordinates graceful shutdown on signals or fatal errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Injects truststore into SSL before other imports and drives shutdown via asyncio signal handlers plus task done-callbacks.

nodes.name: errors.py
nodes.summary: Defines server-specific exception types: GraphLoadError (rich user-facing graph-load failure with formatted traceback and hints) and HealthServerStartupError.

nodes.name: network.py
nodes.summary: Host/port normalization helpers reconciling bracketed IPv6 literals, wildcard bind addresses, and healthcheck target/URL hosts across socket and URL contexts.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Dynamically resolves and imports the configured LangGraph runtime edition (inmem or postgres) from LANGGRAPH_RUNTIME_EDITION and aliases its submodules under server.runtime.*.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses importlib.util.find_spec plus sys.modules aliasing for plugin-style, boot-time backend selection.

nodes.name: metrics.py
nodes.summary: Type-checking-only re-export shim surfacing the active runtime edition's metrics API (e.g. get_metrics) under server.runtime.metrics; the real module is aliased at runtime by the package __init__.

nodes.name: start_python_grpc_server
nodes.summary: Creates the asyncio gRPC server, registers the health, checkpointer, and encryption servicers, binds the insecure port, starts serving, and marks the service healthy.

nodes.name: stop_python_grpc_server
nodes.summary: Gracefully stops the running gRPC server within a grace period and cancels its serving task.

nodes.name: wait_until_python_grpc_ready
nodes.summary: Polls the gRPC health endpoint over an insecure channel until the server reports SERVING or a timeout elapses, raising RuntimeError on failure.

nodes.name: run_python_grpc_server
nodes.summary: Convenience coroutine that starts the gRPC server and then serves forever, logging and re-raising fatal errors.

nodes.name: _parse_metadata
nodes.summary: Parses a proto map of string to JSON-encoded bytes into a Python dict, raising ValueError on any invalid JSON value.

nodes.name: _build_encryption_context
nodes.summary: Constructs an SDK EncryptionContext from proto model, field, and metadata inputs for the encryption handlers.

nodes.name: EncryptionServicerImpl
nodes.summary: gRPC servicer that recursively encrypts and decrypts JSON fields, honoring nested-subfield and skip rules, by delegating to the configured encryption instance's JSON encryptor/decryptor.

nodes.name: CheckpointerServicerImpl
nodes.summary: gRPC servicer implementing the full checkpointer contract (Put, PutWrites, GetCapabilities, List, GetTuple, DeleteThread, DeleteForRuns, CopyThread, Prune) by converting protos to/from LangGraph checkpoint types and delegating to the Python checkpointer.

nodes.name: _ensure_port_available
nodes.summary: Resolves the bind host and probes whether the health-server port can be bound, raising HealthServerStartupError on resolution failure or port conflict.

nodes.name: health_and_metrics_server
nodes.summary: Builds and runs a Starlette app exposing /health and /metrics (Prometheus lines plus pool stats) on a uvicorn server, with pre-bind port checks and error propagation.

nodes.name: entrypoint
nodes.summary: Sets up the logging context, combines lifespans, launches the health/metrics sidecar task, and runs the selected runtime entrypoint until cancelled.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level async driver that installs signal handlers, runs the entrypoint and health-server tasks, and coordinates graceful shutdown and process exit codes.

nodes.name: normalize_host
nodes.summary: Strips surrounding brackets from IPv6 host literals so socket bind/connect APIs receive the raw address.

nodes.name: format_hostport
nodes.summary: Formats a host and port into a connection string, bracketing IPv6 literals.

nodes.name: get_healthcheck_target_host
nodes.summary: Maps a wildcard or IPv6-any bind host to a concrete loopback target address for healthchecks.

nodes.name: get_healthcheck_url_host
nodes.summary: Resolves a host into a URL-safe host component, bracketing IPv6 addresses and mapping wildcard binds to localhost.

nodes.name: GraphLoadError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError raised when a user-provided graph fails to load, capturing the spec, cause, formatted exception-only traceback, and helpful hints for structured logs and user display.

nodes.name: HealthServerStartupError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when the health/metrics server fails to start, such as on a port conflict or bind failure.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server; re-exports the Streamable HTTP routes and documents the stateless, JSON-only transport and the supported protocol versions.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using __all__ to re-export mcp_routes.

nodes.name: _constants.py
nodes.summary: Defines MCP server constants: supported and latest protocol versions, JSON-RPC error codes, and assistant pagination limits shared across the request handlers.

nodes.name: _handlers.py
nodes.summary: Core MCP request handlers for the Streamable HTTP transport: parses and dispatches JSON-RPC messages (initialize, ping, tools/list, tools/call) and maps each LangGraph assistant to an MCP tool.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses functools.lru_cache for a singleton LangGraph client and asyncio.gather to fetch tool schemas concurrently.

nodes.name: _routes.py
nodes.summary: Declares the /mcp route and its HTTP method dispatcher, routing POST to JSON-RPC handling, DELETE to session termination, and rejecting unsupported methods with 405.

nodes.name: _sanitizers.py
nodes.summary: Helpers that adapt LangGraph assistant schemas for MCP consumption: strips LangChain message type definitions from input schemas and normalizes assistant names into spec-compliant MCP tool names.
nodes.languageNotes: Mutates JSON Schema dicts in place and enforces the MCP tool-name charset (^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-.]{1,128}$) via regex.

nodes.name: handle_delete_request
nodes.summary: Handles HTTP DELETE for MCP session termination, returning 404 because this stateless server keeps no session to terminate.

nodes.name: handle_get_request
nodes.summary: Handles HTTP GET for MCP, returning 405 since server-sent-event streaming is not currently supported.

nodes.name: handle_post_request
nodes.summary: Validates and routes an incoming MCP POST body: checks JSON parsing, the Accept header, and JSON-RPC 2.0 framing, then dispatches requests to the JSON-RPC handler or acknowledges responses and notifications.

nodes.name: is_valid_accept_header
nodes.summary: Checks whether the request Accept header advertises application/json or text/event-stream as required by the MCP Streamable HTTP transport.

nodes.name: create_error_response
nodes.summary: Builds a JSON error Response carrying the given message and HTTP status code.

nodes.name: handle_jsonrpc_request
nodes.summary: Dispatches a JSON-RPC request by method (initialize, ping, tools/list, tools/call) and wraps the result or error in a JSON-RPC 2.0 response envelope.

nodes.name: handle_initialize_request
nodes.summary: Handles the MCP initialize request, negotiating the protocol version with the client and returning server capabilities and identity.

nodes.name: handle_tools_list
nodes.summary: Implements MCP tools/list by paging LangGraph assistants, deduplicating them by normalized name, and concurrently building an MCP tool definition from each assistant's input schema.

nodes.name: handle_tools_call
nodes.summary: Implements MCP tools/call by resolving the tool name to a unique assistant, executing a LangGraph run with the supplied arguments, and returning the run result or a tool error.

nodes.name: handle_mcp_endpoint
nodes.summary: Route handler for /mcp that dispatches on HTTP method to the DELETE, GET, or POST handlers and returns 405 for any other method.

nodes.name: simplify_mcp_schema_inplace
nodes.summary: Strips LangChain message type $defs from a LangGraph assistant input schema in place and collapses message-array properties to a single string field for MCP/LLM consumption.

nodes.name: normalize_name
nodes.summary: Converts an assistant name into a valid MCP tool name by collapsing whitespace to underscores, removing disallowed characters, and truncating to 128 characters.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Public API barrel for the vendored lc_security package, re-exporting the SSRF exception, the policy dataclass, the URL/IP/hostname validators, and the SSRF-safe httpx transport and client factory.
nodes.languageNotes: Vendored from langchainplus/lc_security; the module docstring notes it should be deleted in favor of a PyPI dependency once lc-security is published.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Defines SSRFBlockedError, the single exception raised throughout the lc_security package whenever a URL, hostname, or resolved IP is rejected by the SSRF policy.

nodes.name: policy.py
nodes.summary: Core SSRF protection policy defining RFC-annotated blocked IP/CIDR ranges (link-local, private, loopback, cloud-metadata, K8s-internal) plus the scheme, hostname, URL, and resolved-IP validators, including a DNS-aware async entry point.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python's ipaddress module with RFC-annotated CIDR blocklists and extracts IPv4-mapped and NAT64-embedded (RFC 6052) addresses from IPv6 so IPv6 encodings cannot bypass the IPv4 blocklists.

nodes.name: transport.py
nodes.summary: Provides an SSRF-safe httpx async transport that validates scheme, hostname, and every DNS-resolved IP against the policy, then pins the connection to a vetted IP while preserving the Host header and TLS SNI, plus a drop-in AsyncClient factory.
nodes.languageNotes: Subclasses httpx.AsyncBaseTransport and pins the resolved IP via url.copy_with while setting the sni_hostname extension so TLS validation still uses the original hostname, closing the DNS-rebinding window between validation and connection.

nodes.name: SSRFBlockedError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when a request target violates the SSRF policy — a disallowed scheme, blocked hostname, blocked IP range, or a cloud-metadata/Kubernetes-internal endpoint.

nodes.name: SSRFPolicy
nodes.summary: Immutable frozen dataclass configuring which schemes, IP ranges, and hosts are considered safe, with toggles for blocking private IPs, localhost, cloud metadata, and Kubernetes-internal DNS plus additional blocked CIDRs.

nodes.name: SSRFSafeTransport
nodes.summary: httpx.AsyncBaseTransport subclass that guards every outgoing request: it checks scheme and hostname, resolves DNS off-thread, validates all resolved IPs against the SSRF policy, and rewrites the request to the pinned IP while preserving the original Host header and TLS SNI. Re-validates on each redirect hop.

nodes.name: _extract_embedded_ipv4
nodes.summary: Extracts an embedded IPv4 address from IPv4-mapped or NAT64 well-known-prefix (RFC 6052 /96) IPv6 addresses so they can be re-checked against the IPv4 blocklists.

nodes.name: _ip_in_blocked_networks
nodes.summary: Returns a human-readable reason string if an IPv4/IPv6 address falls within any always-blocked, loopback, private, additional-CIDR, or cloud-metadata range per the policy, otherwise None.

nodes.name: validate_resolved_ip
nodes.summary: Parses a resolved IP string and raises SSRFBlockedError if it is blocked, checking the original IPv6 address for blocked networks before extracting and re-checking any embedded IPv4.

nodes.name: validate_hostname
nodes.summary: Raises SSRFBlockedError when a hostname matches localhost, cloud-metadata, or Kubernetes-internal (.svc.cluster.local) patterns that the policy is configured to block.

nodes.name: validate_url
nodes.summary: Primary async URL validator: delegates scheme/hostname/allowed-host checks to validate_url_sync, then resolves DNS off-thread and validates every resolved IP, raising SSRFBlockedError and logging on any violation.

nodes.name: validate_url_sync
nodes.summary: Synchronous URL validator (no DNS) checking scheme, hostname presence, the allowed-hosts bypass, and hostname patterns; suitable for Pydantic validators and other sync contexts.

nodes.name: ssrf_safe_async_client
nodes.summary: Factory that builds an httpx.AsyncClient wired with SSRFSafeTransport, splitting transport-specific kwargs from client kwargs and defaulting to bounded redirect following.

nodes.name: layout.tsx
nodes.summary: Next.js App Router root layout that loads the three brand fonts, runs an inline pre-paint script to restore the saved accent color before hydration, and composes the theme, accent, and URL-state providers around the app with a global toast host.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses next/font/google for zero-layout-shift font loading and an inline dangerouslySetInnerHTML script to set data-accent before hydration, avoiding an accent flash; suppressHydrationWarning covers the theme/accent attribute mismatch.

nodes.name: RootLayout
nodes.summary: Default root layout component rendering the html/body shell with brand font CSS variables and nesting ThemeProvider, AccentProvider, and NuqsAdapter around the page tree.

nodes.name: AccentProvider.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Vitest and React Testing Library suite verifying that AccentProvider falls back to the default accent, hydrates a valid stored accent on mount, ignores invalid stored values, and that setAccent updates state, the html attribute, and localStorage; also asserts useAccent throws outside a provider.

nodes.name: AccentProvider.tsx
nodes.summary: React context provider for the user-selectable accent color, persisting the choice to localStorage (nr-accent) and a data-accent attribute on the html element orthogonally to next-themes light/dark, with an SSR-safe mount-time hydration effect and a guarded useAccent hook.
nodes.languageNotes: Adopts the persisted accent in a mount useEffect rather than a useState initializer to keep SSR and client renders identical and avoid hydration warnings; a type guard validates stored values against the ACCENTS union.

nodes.name: AccentProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider component that tracks the current accent, syncs it from localStorage on mount, and exposes a memoized setAccent callback that updates state, the html data-accent attribute, and storage (swallowing storage failures).

nodes.name: useAccent
nodes.summary: Custom hook returning the accent context value, throwing a descriptive error when called outside an AccentProvider.

nodes.name: ThemeProvider.tsx
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around the next-themes provider configured for class-based light/dark theming that follows the OS by default and persists the user's explicit choice to localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: attribute="class" writes class="dark" on <html> to match Tailwind's class-based dark mode and the .dark token block in globals.css; accent color is handled separately by AccentProvider.

nodes.name: ThemeProvider
nodes.summary: Wrapper component that renders the next-themes provider with class-attribute dark mode, system default, system detection, and transition suppression, forwarding any extra props.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build producing a slim Python 3.13 image for the NextRole backend agent server: stage one resolves the locked uv dependencies into a self-contained /opt/venv, stage two adds the WeasyPrint/Pango PDF native stack and runs uvicorn (server.api.server:app). The single image backs both the `backend` (uvicorn HTTP/SSE) and `core-server` (gRPC data plane) compose services.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build isolates uv dependency resolution (builder) from the runtime; the venv is installed at /opt/venv (outside the /deps/next-role bind mount) so the dev volume overlay never shadows packages, deps are resolved from uv.lock only (uv virtual project, no build backend), and a uv cache mount speeds rebuilds. PYTHONPATH spans repo-root and backend/ to resolve both agent and server package imports.

nodes.name: builder
nodes.summary: Builder stage (python:3.13-slim) that installs uv and resolves the frozen uv.lock dependencies into a self-contained, bytecode-precompiled virtualenv at /opt/venv, with build-essential available as insurance for any sdist fallback.

nodes.name: python
nodes.summary: Runtime stage (python:3.13-slim) that installs the WeasyPrint/Pango/HarfBuzz PDF rendering stack, copies the prebuilt venv from the builder, bakes the backend source at /deps/next-role/backend, exposes port 8000, and launches uvicorn with graceful-shutdown and keep-alive tuning.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Docker build-context exclusions for the backend image: drops the local virtualenv, Python bytecode and tooling caches, build/coverage artifacts, secrets (.env files), and all user-uploaded and agent-generated content (uploads, research, tailored resumes, battlecards, conversation history) so PII and dev cruft never enter image layers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `**/` globs because the excluded content directories live under agents/career_agent/ while the build context root is backend/ (bare names would only match at the context root); mirrors backend/.gitignore to keep PII out of both git history and image layers.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Single-stage development container image for the Next.js frontend on node:24-alpine that enables pnpm via corepack, installs dependencies from a frozen lockfile, exposes port 3000, and runs `pnpm dev` bound to 0.0.0.0 for hot-reload inside the local Docker Compose stack.
nodes.languageNotes: Dependency layer is cached by copying package.json + pnpm-lock.yaml before the full source; this is a dev image (CMD runs `pnpm dev` for Turbopack hot-reload) rather than a multi-stage production build.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Excludes node_modules, build output (.next/dist), tool caches (.turbo, tsbuildinfo, eslintcache), env files except .env.example, and OS/IDE cruft from the frontend Docker build context, keeping the image lean and preventing secrets from entering the container.

nodes.name: ci.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions code-quality workflow for PRs and pushes to main: a cheap detect-code job runs dorny/paths-filter, then a gated code-quality job runs the pre-commit linters (ruff lint/format, ty backend types, eslint + prettier + tsc frontend) only when non-doc files change.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the always-trigger + job-level `if:` pattern so the required `code-quality` check is always reported; a workflow-level paths-ignore skip would leave the required check stuck in 'Expected' and block the merge. Its SKIP list partitions the pre-commit hooks with hygiene.yml so hooks never run twice.

nodes.name: backend-tests.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow running the backend unit-test suite (uv sync + uv run pytest) on PRs and pushes to main, gated by a detect-backend paths-filter so the expensive job runs only when backend or workflow files change.
nodes.languageNotes: Same required-check gating pattern — the job is skipped (reports success) via a job-level `if:` rather than a workflow-level paths filter. Integration tests requiring a live Postgres/Redis stack run separately.

nodes.name: frontend-tests.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow running the frontend Vitest unit + component suite (pnpm test, node + jsdom projects) on PRs and pushes to main, gated by a detect-frontend paths-filter so the job runs only when frontend or workflow files change.
nodes.languageNotes: Same required-check gating pattern as the other CI workflows; installs Node via frontend/.nvmrc and pnpm with a frozen lockfile.

nodes.name: hygiene.yml
nodes.summary: Lightweight GitHub Actions workflow running file-hygiene pre-commit hooks and gitleaks secret scanning on every change (docs included) via uvx, with the code linters SKIP-ped so they run only in the code-quality pipeline.
nodes.languageNotes: Runs pre-commit through uvx with no backend/frontend toolchain install; its SKIP list is the complement of ci.yml's, partitioning the hook set so each hook runs exactly once.

nodes.name: 000001_init.up.sql
nodes.summary: Consolidated (squashed) initial migration defining the complete NextRole Agent Server database schema: 11 tables backing LangGraph assistants, threads, runs, checkpoints, cron, and the KV store, plus the btree_gin/ltree/pgvector extensions, indexes, sequences, and foreign keys. Applied once at backend boot under a Redis lock.
nodes.languageNotes: Squashed single-file schema. Run and checkpoint tables intentionally omit foreign keys (write-path lock avoidance, async GC); the run table doubles as a work queue with partial indexes on status='pending'/'running' for index-only queue claims, and thread carries partial expression indexes over metadata->>'owner'/'ls_user_id' for multi-user isolation.

nodes.name: assistant
nodes.summary: Stores LangGraph assistant definitions keyed by assistant_id: graph_id, versioned config/metadata/context (jsonb), and timestamps. Root entity referenced by assistant_versions, cron, and run.

nodes.name: assistant_versions
nodes.summary: Version history for assistants keyed by (assistant_id, version), capturing each revision's graph_id, config, metadata, and context. Cascades on assistant deletion.

nodes.name: checkpoint_blobs
nodes.summary: Stores LangGraph checkpoint channel values as binary blobs keyed by (thread_id, checkpoint_ns, channel, version). Part of the Postgres checkpointer persistence layer.

nodes.name: checkpoint_delete_queue
nodes.summary: Async garbage-collection queue for checkpoint deletions, with a bigint id backed by a sequence and a unique dedup index on (thread_id, checkpoint_ns, checkpoint_id).

nodes.name: checkpoint_writes
nodes.summary: Stores intermediate checkpoint writes per task keyed by (thread_id, checkpoint_ns, checkpoint_id, task_id, idx). Feeds the LangGraph checkpointer's write path.

nodes.name: checkpoints
nodes.summary: Core LangGraph checkpoint table holding serialized graph state (jsonb) per thread, keyed by (thread_id, checkpoint_ns, checkpoint_id), with optional run_id and parent_checkpoint_id lineage links.

nodes.name: cron
nodes.summary: Scheduled-run (cron) definitions with schedule, next_run_date, payload, and enabled flag; references assistant and thread with cascade delete, guarded by a CHECK on on_run_completed and a partial index on next_run_date where enabled.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Run records that double as the agent work queue: status ('pending'/'running'/...), kwargs, and multitask_strategy, with partial covering indexes enabling index-only queue claims and aggressive autovacuum tuning for high write churn.

nodes.name: store
nodes.summary: General-purpose key-value store keyed by (prefix, key) holding jsonb values with optional TTL/expiry; backs LangGraph's long-term memory store, with a partial index on non-null expires_at for expiry sweeps.

nodes.name: thread
nodes.summary: Conversation/execution thread state keyed by thread_id: status, config, values (jsonb graph state), interrupts, and TTL strategy. Central entity with GIN indexes on metadata/values and partial expression indexes over metadata owner/ls_user_id for multi-user isolation.

nodes.name: thread_ttl
nodes.summary: Per-thread TTL policies with a STORED generated expires_at column (created_at + ttl_minutes) and a unique (thread_id, strategy) index; cascades on thread deletion to drive thread expiry/GC.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template listing every runtime environment variable — LLM provider API keys, Postgres/Redis/object-store connection settings, host port mappings, and opt-in multi-user auth secrets — that developers copy to `.env` for local setup.
nodes.languageNotes: Flat KEY=value template covering 39 variables; the AWS_* keys are reserved for Bedrock while the object store reads only OBJECT_STORE_* — a documented separation.

nodes.name: .pre-commit-config.yaml
nodes.summary: Pre-commit hook configuration enforcing the CI quality gate: whitespace/EOF fixers, gitleaks secret scanning, backend ruff lint+format and ty type-check, and frontend eslint/prettier/type-check plus a LangChain SDK version-sync check.
nodes.languageNotes: Mixes pinned remote hook repos with a `local` repo of system-language hooks that `cd` into backend/frontend so each tool discovers its own config; generated proto stubs are excluded because pre-commit passes filenames explicitly.

nodes.languageNotes: Uses depends_on condition gating (service_healthy / service_completed_successfully) plus per-service healthchecks to order boot, and ${VAR:-default} interpolation to keep multi-user auth opt-in; bind-mounts the repo while shadowing .venv/node_modules with anonymous volumes.

nodes.name: object-store

nodes.name: config.yml

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Entry-point overview of the NextRole backend, describing the agents-vs-agent-server split, the two compose services (backend and core-server), the folder layout, and how to run and develop the stack with uv.

nodes.name: pyproject.toml

nodes.name: CAREER_AGENT.md
nodes.summary: Operational memory loaded into the career agent's system prompt, detailing the multi-stage workflow (intake, document processing, hiring-recon research, resume tailoring + interview prep, battlecard generation, and multi-turn updates) with concrete tool calls, slug-naming rules, and subagent-routing task templates.
nodes.languageNotes: Serves as always-on agent memory rather than human-facing docs; each stage prescribes exact tool invocations (parse_document, extract_jd, task, render_battlecard_pdf) and file-path conventions.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Architecture README for the career agent, explaining its filesystem-primitive design (memory, skills, subagents), the five-stage delegation flow, multi-turn update routing, file-upload handling, and the object-store file layout for processed inputs and rendered artifacts.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents the deepagents pattern of configuring an agent through filesystem primitives — memory (markdown), skills (SKILL.md), and subagents (YAML) — with subagents loaded via a load_subagents() helper.

nodes.name: subagents.yaml
nodes.summary: Declarative definitions for the career agent's three delegated subagents — hiring-recon, resume-tailor, and interview-coach — specifying each one's model, tools, skills path, and single-shot system prompt with strict one-line reply contracts.
nodes.languageNotes: Externalizes subagent config to YAML so deepagents builds a dedicated SkillsMiddleware per subagent; each system_prompt enforces create-vs-update mode and an exact 'Wrote/Updated … to/at: <path>' final line.

nodes.name: 01_file_upload.md
nodes.summary: PRD for the career-agent file-upload feature: the frontend writes CV/JD bytes directly to the agent's on-disk /upload/ path (no Python endpoint) via the Workspace Files surface, with hover/dialog deletion sharing one confirmation.

nodes.name: 02_document_processing.md
nodes.summary: PRD for document processing: uploaded CVs/JDs are parsed to clean markdown in /processed/ via LlamaParse, with the tool persisting files itself (no LLM re-emit), upsert semantics, and robustness follow-ups for parse-failure recovery.

nodes.name: 03_jd_url_extraction.md
nodes.summary: PRD for extracting job descriptions from a URL: a dedicated extract_jd tool fetches the page via Tavily and persists /processed/<slug>.md, mirroring parse_document, with auth-wall detection and recovery paths.

nodes.name: 04_initialize_subagents.md
nodes.summary: PRD introducing career-agent Stages 3-5: the hiring-recon, resume-tailor, and interview-coach subagents plus the interview-battlecard skill, with per-stage delegation procedures and the nested <resume>/<jd> artifact layout.

nodes.name: 05_chat_streaming_throttle.md
nodes.summary: PRD for a conditional 80ms chat-streaming throttle plus identity-stable caching and pointer-event click hardening to keep the UI responsive during parallel subagent tool-arg streaming (headline throttle later superseded by the @langchain/react migration).

nodes.name: 06_subagents_with_skills.md
nodes.summary: PRD refactoring the three career-agent subagents to load their workflow from per-consumer SKILL.md files, collapsing inline system prompts to ~7-line pointers so workflows become reusable units.

nodes.name: 07_tailored_resume_pdf.md
nodes.summary: PRD for rendering the tailored resume as a rendercv YAML source-of-truth plus generated PDF, via a three-step subagent flow (write YAML, prepare_render_settings, execute rendercv) and a tool-wiring refactor.

nodes.name: 08_agent_workflow.md
nodes.summary: Consolidated v2 PRD documenting the end-to-end 5-stage career-agent workflow orchestration, folding in the skills and rendercv follow-ups; the canonical reference for how intake through battlecard runs today.

nodes.name: 09_file_category_colors.md
nodes.summary: PRD for color-coding Workspace file cards by root folder category using CSS-variable icon tints, a purely frontend mapping derived from the virtual path string.

nodes.name: 10_print_file_as_pdf.md
nodes.summary: PRD for a Print button in the file preview dialog that renders content into a hidden same-origin iframe at /print/file and calls window.print(), using the OS Save-as-PDF instead of a PDF library.

nodes.name: 11_interview_battlecard_pdf.md
nodes.summary: PRD for rendering the interview battlecard as a JSON source-of-truth plus weasyprint-generated PDF via a one-step in-process render tool with bundled jinja2/CSS/FiraSans template assets.

nodes.name: 12_multi_turn_updates.md
nodes.summary: PRD enabling multi-turn in-place updates to career-agent artifacts, routing edits to each artifact's owning subagent (or the main agent for the battlecard) with a create-vs-update reply-verb contract and user-overridable preservation defaults.

nodes.name: 13_execute_virtual_path_translation.md
nodes.summary: PRD adding a VirtualPathShellBackend subclass that rewrites /-prefixed virtual paths to on-disk paths inside the execute tool, giving the agent a single path convention end-to-end for rendercv commands.

nodes.name: 14_multi_select_file_delete.md
nodes.summary: PRD for Drive-style multi-select delete of Workspace files: hover checkboxes, shift-click ranges, Cmd+A/Esc, a frontend fan-out removeFiles helper with a single refresh, and a unified 1-or-many confirmation dialog.

nodes.name: 15_configurable_llm_models.md
nodes.summary: PRD making the main-agent and subagent LLM models configurable at runtime via a wrap_model_call middleware reading configurable.*_model, wired into both the main agent and every declarative subagent, with a Settings UI section.

nodes.name: 16_disable_subagent_streaming.md
nodes.summary: PRD disabling token streaming on the subagent model to work around an O(n^2) per-token concat in the LangGraph SDK, folded into the model-override middleware behind a reversible default-on toggle (later flipped off after the @langchain/react migration).

nodes.name: 17_preference_memory.md
nodes.summary: PRD for long-term user-preference memory: a single always-loaded /memory/preferences.md seeded by a before_agent hook and edited by the main agent, applied by folding preferences into subagent task inputs.

nodes.name: 18_langchain_react_migration.md
nodes.summary: PRD migrating the frontend chat surface to the @langchain/react v2 stream runtime (eliminating the SDK's O(n^2) concat) and re-enabling live subagent streaming, keeping the toggle as a rollback lever.

nodes.name: 19_bulk_delete_dialog_full_paths.md
nodes.summary: PRD fixing the bulk-delete confirmation dialog to key and render rows by full virtual path instead of colliding basenames, resolving a React duplicate-key warning and disambiguating same-named files.

nodes.name: 20_ui_modernization.md
nodes.summary: PRD for a whole-UI modernization to a warm paper+espresso theme with logo-matched emerald accent, next-themes light/dark toggle, selectable accents, a tool-activity timeline rail, and clickable file paths - a restyle over frozen streaming logic.

nodes.name: 21_own_agent_server.md
nodes.summary: PRD recording the rationale and key decisions behind replacing the closed langchain/langgraph-api base image with an in-repo, self-owned Python agent server (backend/server/) split into a hot-reloading API service and a Python core-server data plane.
nodes.languageNotes: Structured PRD with fixed sections (Why / What the user sees / How / Files of interest / Decisions / Deferred / Verify); a repo-wide convention across docs/prd/.

nodes.name: 23_progressive_tool_disclosure.md

nodes.name: 24_collapsible_threads_panel.md
nodes.summary: PRD describing the unification of thread navigation into a single always-mounted collapsible Threads panel (docked on desktop, scrim overlay on mobile) with local open/pinned state, replacing the earlier drawer-plus-dock split.

nodes.name: 25_object_storage_artifacts.md

nodes.name: 26_multi_user_auth.md

tour.title: Product Overview & Workflow
tour.description: Start with the README to learn what NextRole is: upload a CV and a job description, and a multi-agent system with long-term memory produces a tailored resume PDF, a researched interview-prep doc, and a day-of battlecard. It lays out the supervisor-plus-subagents architecture, the DeepAgents memory/storage model, and the Python/Next.js tech stack. CAREER_AGENT.md then shows the concrete end-to-end workflow the product runs — intake, document processing, hiring recon, resume tailoring plus interview prep, and battlecard generation — the storyline the rest of this tour follows through the code.

tour.title: Frontend Entry & App Shell
tour.description: The frontend is a Next.js 16 App Router application. layout.tsx is the root layout: it loads the brand fonts, runs a pre-paint script to restore the saved accent color before hydration, and wraps the app in theme, accent, and URL-state providers. page.tsx is the application shell rendered inside it — bootstrapping config, constructing the LangGraph assistant/client, and laying out the resizable chat-plus-workspace surface with a threads panel. This is where every UI concern in the following steps gets composed together.
tour.languageLesson: Next.js App Router splits a route into a layout (the persistent shell, rendered once and kept across navigations) and a page (the route's content). Both are React Server Components by default; files that need browser APIs or hooks opt in with a top-of-file "use client" directive.

tour.title: Client & Chat Providers
tour.description: Building on the providers wired in Step 2, this is the client-side integration layer that connects the UI to the backend agent server. ClientProvider builds a memoized LangGraph SDK client and attaches a bearer-token hook so every request carries auth in multi-user mode; ChatProvider instantiates the useChat hook and shares its state via context. useChat is the heart of the integration — it drives the @langchain/react v2 stream runtime and keeps agent files synchronized across graph state, the SDK store, and artifacts. config.ts holds the deployment URL, assistant id, and model preferences, pinning them to env in auth mode as an XSS guard.
tour.languageLesson: The @langchain/react v2 useStream runtime consumes the agent server's Server-Sent Events and exposes messages, tool calls, and interrupts as reactive React state — so the UI re-renders incrementally as tokens and events arrive, with no manual SSE parsing in components.

tour.title: Rendering the Chat Stream
tour.description: With the stream flowing from Step 3, these components render it. ChatInterface is the primary chat surface — the conversation stream plus the composer, with file drag-and-drop and first-run upload cues. ChatMessage renders one turn by composing its parts: markdown content, grouped tool calls, and subagent cards. ToolCallGroup collapses a message's tool calls into a section and wires per-call human approval interrupts, while SubagentCard visualizes a delegated subagent's live execution — status, tool batches, duration, and streamed output. Together they make the multi-agent system's work legible to the user.

tour.title: Workspace & Artifact Uploads
tour.description: The right-hand workspace is where the products of the agent live. Workspace aggregates the agent's plan, generated files, and researched sources (merging sources from the main agent and its subagents), and TasksFilesSidebar lists artifact files with multi-select, bulk delete, and drag-and-drop upload. useFileUpload manages the upload UX, uploadFiles posts CV/JD bytes as multipart form data to the backend artifact files API, and agentFiles unifies three file sources — graph state, the SDK store, and that artifacts API — into one shape. This upload path is exactly what the backend object-storage layer in Step 8 serves.

tour.title: The Career Agent Core
tour.description: Now cross the HTTP boundary into the backend. agents.py wires the career deep-agent: it builds a CompositeBackend (a virtual-path shell, key/value store routes, and object-store artifact routes), constructs the tool set, and assembles the main supervisor agent plus its declarative subagents. prompts.py is the central prompt library, including override constants that replace the DeepAgents/LangChain defaults at import time, and middleware.py injects the current date, seeds the always-loaded preferences memory file, and applies per-run model overrides. The career-agent README explains the guiding idea: memory, skills, and subagents are all modeled as a filesystem the agent reads and writes.
tour.languageLesson: DeepAgents builds on LangGraph by giving the agent filesystem-like primitives (files, todos, skills, subagents) instead of ad-hoc tools. A CompositeBackend routes different virtual path prefixes to different storage implementations, so the same read/write tools transparently hit local scratch space, a KV store, or object storage depending on the path.

tour.title: Tools, Subagents & Templates
tour.description: These are the capabilities the agent from Step 6 delegates to. tools.py defines the toolset — Tavily web search/extract, artifact file operations, LlamaParse document parsing, JD-URL extraction, and the two PDF render pipelines (rendercv for resumes, WeasyPrint for battlecards). subagents.yaml declaratively defines the three specialists — hiring-recon, resume-tailor, and interview-coach — each with its own model, tools, skill path, and strict one-line reply contract. The resume-tailor SKILL.md shows how a subagent's behavior is authored as a Markdown skill (workflow, a truth-vs-tailoring guardrail, and the rendercv YAML contract), and battlecard.html.j2 is the template that lays out the final battlecard for PDF conversion.
tour.languageLesson: Jinja2 (.j2) templates interleave HTML with {% for %} loops and {{ variable }} substitutions. Rendering structured data to HTML and then converting to PDF (via WeasyPrint) keeps layout in a designer-friendly template rather than hard-coded in Python.

tour.title: Object-Storage Artifact Layer
tour.description: This layer persists the binary artifacts the agent produces and the uploads from Step 5. object_storage.py owns the single source of truth for mapping virtual paths to object-store keys; object_backend.py implements the DeepAgents backend protocol over S3-compatible storage (SeaweedFS locally); and shell_backend.py rewrites virtual-path tokens in shell commands to real on-disk paths while stripping secrets from the subprocess environment. scope.py derives per-user store namespaces and object-key prefixes from the run's identity, and files_api.py is the Starlette HTTP API (list/read/upload/write/delete) that Step 5's uploadFiles client talks to — mounted into the agent server and scoped to the request user.
tour.languageLesson: Modeling remote object storage behind the same backend protocol as the local filesystem means the agent's file tools don't know or care where bytes live. Centralizing the path-to-key mapping in one module (used by both the agent backend and the HTTP API) guarantees the two never disagree about where an artifact is stored.

tour.title: Agent Server: API & Streaming
tour.description: The career agent runs inside a vendored LangGraph agent server. server.py builds the Starlette ASGI application — applying middleware, merging the OpenAPI spec, and mounting routes. runs.py exposes the run lifecycle the frontend drives: create, stream, wait, join, and cancel. stream_v2.py normalizes graph output into the v2 protocol event stream that Step 3's useChat consumes, and event_streaming/service.py defines the ThreadRunManager that orchestrates a thread's streaming session over both SSE and WebSocket transports.
tour.languageLesson: Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream a long-lived HTTP response as incremental data: frames — a natural fit for token-by-token LLM output. The v2 protocol layer wraps raw graph events into typed protocol messages so the client can distinguish tokens, tool calls, and interrupts on one connection.

tour.title: Runtime & Data Plane
tour.description: Behind the API sits the persistence and execution machinery. checkpoint.py is the Postgres-backed LangGraph checkpoint saver that durably records graph state so runs can resume and be inspected; store.py is the long-term key/value store (with pgvector indexes and Redis-coordinated TTL) that backs the agent's memory; and queue.py dispatches queued runs to a pool of background worker threads with completion webhooks and graceful drain. core_server/__main__.py boots a second process — an async gRPC data-plane server that implements some RPCs natively and transparently forwards the rest — and db.py manages its shared psycopg3 connection pool.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph checkpointing persists a snapshot of graph state after each step. This is what makes agent runs durable (survive restarts), resumable after a human-approval interrupt, and inspectable — the same mechanism that lets Step 4's approval interrupts pause and later continue a run.

tour.title: The Database Schema
tour.description: The runtime from Step 10 persists into this Postgres schema. A single squashed migration defines all 11 tables — assistants, threads, runs, checkpoints, cron, and the KV store — plus the btree_gin, ltree, and pgvector extensions, applied once at boot under a Redis lock. Notice how the design reuses tables for more than storage: the checkpoints table holds serialized graph state keyed by thread, the thread table carries partial expression indexes over owner/user id for multi-user isolation, and the run table doubles as the agent work queue that Step 10's dispatcher claims from. init.sql is the one-time bootstrap that enables pgvector before the migration runs.
tour.languageLesson: Versioned SQL migrations evolve the schema in ordered, idempotent steps; squashing many historical migrations into one initial file keeps a fresh install fast, while a boot-time lock (here via Redis) ensures only one process applies it. The pgvector extension adds a vector column type and similarity indexes, enabling semantic search over the agent's stored memory.

tour.title: Multi-User Authentication
tour.description: Authentication is opt-in, and when enabled it spans both apps. On the frontend, auth/server.ts is the Better Auth identity provider (Postgres-backed, email/password plus optional Google OAuth) that mints short-lived EdDSA-signed JWTs; auth/client.ts is the browser client that obtains them; auth/token.ts caches, refreshes, and injects the bearer token into every SDK request (the hook Step 3's ClientProvider attaches); and SessionGate.tsx redirects unauthenticated visitors to /login. On the backend, agents/auth.py verifies that JWT against the published JWKS and stamps per-user ownership onto threads, crons, assistants, and store namespaces — enforcing the isolation Step 11's indexes support, with a global default-deny.
tour.languageLesson: EdDSA is asymmetric: the frontend signs JWTs with a private key, and the backend verifies them using the public key published at a JWKS endpoint — so the backend never shares a secret with the issuer and can validate tokens statelessly. The token's sub claim carries the user id that becomes the per-user scope prefix from Step 8.

tour.title: Containerization & CI
tour.description: Finally, how it all runs and ships. docker-compose.yml orchestrates the seven-service local stack — frontend, the uvicorn backend agent server, the gRPC core-server, Postgres+pgvector, Redis, SeaweedFS object storage, and a one-shot bucket-init job — with health checks and dependency-ordered boot. The backend Dockerfile is a multi-stage build that resolves locked dependencies into a venv and adds the WeasyPrint/Pango native stack for PDF rendering; a single image backs both the HTTP and gRPC services from Steps 9-10. The frontend Dockerfile builds the Next.js dev image, and ci.yml gates every PR on the pre-commit quality suite (ruff, ty, eslint, prettier, tsc), running only when non-doc files change.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use one stage to compile/resolve dependencies and a second, slimmer stage that copies only the runtime artifacts — shrinking the final image and keeping build tools out of production. Docker Compose depends_on with health checks orders service startup so, for example, the backend waits for Postgres to be ready.
